https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Set the background and add a new sprite for player one.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**Game design starts with choosing the background and the players.**

1.  Connect a `set background` block to `when run`

2.  Add a new sprite and choose a costume for player one

3.  Press `Run`

authored_hints.hint1: Connect a set background block under When run.
authored_hints.hint2:
Select a background from the dropdown menu

[blockly block][0]
authored_hints.hint3: Connect a make new sprite block under When run.

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Make a player two sprite with a make new sprite block. Then set the location using the location pin.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions: **Add more sprites and set their location.** 1. Make another new sprite and choose a costume for player two 2. Click the location pin to move the new sprite 3. Press `Run`

authored_hints.hint1: The location block is attached to the make new sprite block. To change a sprite's location, click the pin on the location block, then click where you want it to go. [][0]
authored_hints.Hint2:
You can choose the costume from the dropdown menu.

[][0]

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Add the new block to your program. Click on the grid to create place sprites exactly where you want them.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions: **Making Lots of Sprites with a Grid** 1. Add the `make sprites using grid` block 2. **Click on the grid** to choose exactly where you want to place lots of sprites

authored_hints.Hint1:
Click on the grid after you move the block into your workspace.

Then select the boxes where you want the sprite to go. 

Click off the grid when you are finished.

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Try out some sprite behaviors! Press run to see them in action. Try different behaviors if you like.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**Try it: Animated sprites using behaviors!** 

Behaviors are actions that happen over and over while the program runs.

1.  Look over the code.
2.  Press `Run` to see the sprites in action!

* * *

**Bonus:** Try choosing a few different behaviors!

authored_hints.hint1:
You can choose a new behavior from the dropdown menu.

[blockly block][0]

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Make one sprite chase the other. Add `moving with arrow keys` behavior to a sprite.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**Set up a game of chase using behavior blocks.**

1.  Code the dragon sprites to `begin moving with arrow keys`.

2.  Press `Run` and use the arrows to get moving!

authored_hints.hint: Connect two sprite begins behavior block to your program. One for each of the sprites.
authored_hints.hint2: Choose the wandering behavior from the dropdown menu of one behavior and moving with arrow keys from the other. [blockly block][0]

callouts.arrows: Move the sprite with the arrows!

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Add the new block to your program. Click on the grid to create place sprites exactly where you want them.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**Change the behavior of many sprites.**

1.  Add the `sprite begins behavior` block

2.  Press `Run`. What do you notice? 

* * *

**Bonus:** You can change the behavior if you would like.

Try adding this block to your program.

panels.game_design_panel_events-adeb522e-b960-4:
### Using Events

Events do not snap into your main program.

They create little programs of their own.
panels.game_design_panel_events-820d9730-1582-4:
#### When Sprite Clicked

The computer waits for the user to click. 

When there is a click, it executes the code under the event.

[][0]
panels.game_design_panel_events-b0361be1-a7c8-4:
#### When sprite touches sprite

The computer waits for the sprites to be touching. 

As soon as they touch, it executes the code under the event. 

[][0]
panels.game_design_panel_events-068701d9-edca-4: #### Event Toolbox Click the the + to open the event toolbox. You can use the blocks from the toolbox when you have a group of sprite but only want the action to apply to one sprite at a time.

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Make your sprite grow when you click on it.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**Make your sprite grow when you click it.**
1. Connect a `change size` block under the event `when sprite clicked` 2. Click on the sprite after you press `Run`

authored_hints.hint1: Connect a sprite change size block to the when sprite touches sprite event. [blockly block][0]

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Write a program to change the size of each sprite when it is clicked.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions: **Events with Sprite Groups** 1. Click the + to open the event toolbox 2. Drag the `clicked sprite` block into the `change size` block below 3. Click on each sprite to change their size after pressing Run.

authored_hints.expand_click_event:
Click the `+` button on the event block. Use the block inside to finish the program.

[blockly block][0]

callouts.mini-toolbox-blue-button: Click the blue button to open or close.

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Code your program so that clicking on a sprite removes it.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions: **Collect one sprite at a time.** 1. Click the the + to open the event toolbox 2. Drag down the `clicked sprite` and connect it to `remove` 3. Tap on each sprite after clicking `Run`.

authored_hints.groups_click_hint:
You will need an event block. Make sure to click your sprites after pressing "Run!"

[blockly block][0]
authored_hints.groups_say_hint:
Add a `clicked` block to a `remove` block. You can find it inside the event block!

[blockly block][0]

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Code a sound to play when the two sprites touch.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**What happens when sprites touch?** Try a new type of event.

1.  Add a `play sound` block to the `when sprites touch` event

2.  Press `Run` and watch what happens when the sprites touch!

authored_hints.hint1:
Connect a play sound under the when sprite touches sprite event block:
[blockly block][0]

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Change the costume of a sprite when they touch.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions: **Remove a sprite when they touch.** 1. Click the the + to open the event toolbox 2. Drag the `object` egg block down to the `remove` block 3. Press `Run` then use the arrows to move the ramen sprite

Click the `+` button on the event block. Use the `object` block inside to finish the program.

dsls.description: Great work so far! Choose your next activity. Complete as many as you can. Keep practicing with events and groups of sprites!

game_design_practice_lots_choice_a_indep.bubble_choice_description: Make the sprites move when they are clicked.
game_design_practice_lots_choice_a_indep.short_instructions: Make the sprites move when they are clicked.
game_design_practice_lots_choice_a_indep.long_instructions:
**Finish the game.** Make the sprites move when they are clicked.
![expandable](https://images.code.org/371f4ba22b3a2b81d63da7059d84590a-image-1641824534552.2022-01-10 09_21_44.gif)
1. Drag out the `when sprite clicked` event and open the toolbox `+` - Make the `object sprite` begin a behavior when clicked - Press `Run` then click each sprite *** ***Note:*** *This code starts with three `make new sprite` blocks so that the location of each could be set to an exact spot.* *You could use the `make 3 new sprites` block if you don't mind a random starting location.*

game_design_practice_lots_choice_b_indep.bubble_choice_description: Finish the game and help the bunny collect the carrots.
game_design_practice_lots_choice_b_indep.short_instructions: Remove the carrot sprites when the bunny touches them.
game_design_practice_lots_choice_b_indep.long_instructions:
**Finish this game!** Make the carrot sprites get removed when the bunny touches them.
![expandable](https://images.code.org/dd88bcd240491e58f95fcb2127ad181c-image-1641827026540.2022-01-10 10_02_39.gif)
1. Use a `when touches` event and a `remove` block to finish the game. *Extra Challenge:* Change the size of the bunny sprite each time a carrot is touched.

game_design_practice_lots_choice_c_indep.bubble_choice_description: Change the color of sprites using events.
game_design_practice_lots_choice_c_indep.short_instructions: Change the color of the sprites using events.
game_design_practice_lots_choice_c_indep.long_instructions:
**Finish the game!** Change the color of the sprites using events.
![expandable](https://images.code.org/39306839858f0b3a39b05463f99755bb-image-1641584970345.2022-01-07 14_48_51.gif)
1. Use a `when sprite touches sprite` event - Change the color of the `object sprite` when they touch another sprite - Press `Run` and play! *** **Extra Challenge:** Can you make the sprites change to a *random color* each time?

game_design_practice_lots_choice_d_indep.bubble_choice_description: Remove the sprites when they are clicked.
game_design_practice_lots_choice_d_indep.long_instructions:
**Finish the game!** Remove the sprites using events.
![expandable](https://images.code.org/137b0a8b6261d07e40cbacbc75c28335-image-1641587649737.2022-01-07 15_33_36.gif)
1. Create a program that makes at least 3 sprites. - Then, each sprite is removed when it is clicked. *** **Extra Challenge:** Make (and remove) two or more different kinds of sprites.

behavior_descriptions.move a sprite, changing its direction ra: move a sprite, changing its direction randomly
behavior_descriptions.move a sprite to the right across the sc: move a sprite to the right across the screen
behavior_descriptions.rotate a sprite to its left: rotate a sprite to its left
behavior_descriptions.rotate a sprite to its right: rotate a sprite to its right
behavior_descriptions.change the size of a sprite: change the size of a sprite
behavior_descriptions.move a sprite downwards across the scree: move a sprite downwards across the screen
behavior_descriptions.move a sprite horizontally, reversing di: move a sprite horizontally, reversing direction if it touches the edges of the screen
behavior_descriptions.move a sprite upwards across the screen: move a sprite upwards across the screen
behavior_descriptions.randomly change the size of a sprite: randomly change the size of a sprite
behavior_descriptions.move a sprite to the left across the scr: move a sprite to the left across the screen
behavior_descriptions.move a sprite across the screen, reversi: move a sprite across the screen, reversing direction if it touches the edges
behavior_descriptions.move a sprite if the user is pressing th: move a sprite if the user is pressing the arrow keys
behavior_descriptions.randomly change the vertical position of: randomly change the vertical position of a sprite
behavior_descriptions.randomly set the rotation of a sprite: randomly set the rotation of a sprite
behavior_descriptions.Move a sprite to the left across the scr: Move a sprite to the left across the screen. When it goes off the left side of the screen, move it back to the right side of the screen.
behavior_descriptions.Move a sprite to the right across the sc: Move a sprite to the right across the screen. When it goes off the right side of the screen, move it back to the left side of the screen.
behavior_descriptions.Move a sprite up across the screen. When: Move a sprite up across the screen. When it goes off the top of the screen, move it back to the bottom of the screen.

game_design_practice_lots_choice_e_indep.bubble_choice_description: Practice changing each sprite's costume using events.
game_design_practice_lots_choice_e_indep.short_instructions: Practice changing each sprite's costume using events.
game_design_practice_lots_choice_e_indep.long_instructions:
**Finish the game!** Practice changing each sprite's costume using events.
![expandable](https://images.code.org/29dc89dbaa522a5cc418620013717af4-image-1641833820697.2022-01-10 11_55_38.gif)
1. Make at least 3 sprites with the same costume - Make each sprite change costumes when it is clicked - Press `Run` and try it out **Extra challenge:** Create another group of sprites that all change costume _**at the same time**_ when one is clicked.

behavior_descriptions.Move a sprite to the left across the scr: Move a sprite to the left across the screen. When it goes off the left side of the screen, move it back to the right side of the screen.
behavior_descriptions.Move a sprite to the right across the sc: Move a sprite to the right across the screen. When it goes off the right side of the screen, move it back to the left side of the screen.

dsls.description:
**Try out one of these simple games!** How does the scoring work? When you are ready to code your own scoreboard click Finish!

The spicy peppers indicate how difficult the game is to win. 

🌶️: Mild and easy 

🌶️🌶️: Medium with some spice

🌶️🌶️🌶️: Spicy! Difficult! 

🌶️🌶️🌶️🌶️: Extra hot! Can you handle it?

game_variables_intro_choices_a_independe.bubble_choice_description:
**Difficulty:** 🌶️

**Game Mechanic:** Collect  

**How to win:** Use the arrows to get 6 arctic jewels.
game_variables_intro_choices_a_independe.long_instructions:
**Play the game!**
 
 - Press `Run` and play the game. 
 
 - What do you notice?
 - Press `Finish` to play another game or move on.

authored_hints.Hint1: Press the `Run` button then use the arrow keys to move the fox to the jewels.

game_variables_intro_choices_b_independe.bubble_choice_description:
**Difficulty:** 🌶️

**Game Mechanic:** Moving & Collecting 

**How to win:** Use the arrows to catch 10 stars
game_variables_intro_choices_b_independe.long_instructions:
**Play the game!**
 
 - Press `Run` and play the game. 
 
 - What do you notice?
 - Press `Finish` to play another game or move on.

game_variables_intro_choices_c_independe.bubble_choice_description:
**Difficulty:** 🌶️🌶️

**Game Mechanics:** Click & Collect 

**How to win:** Click on 12 of the bouncing balls!
game_variables_intro_choices_c_independe.long_instructions:
**Play the game!**
 
 - Press `Run` and play the game. 
 
 - What do you notice?
 - Press `Finish` to play another game or move on.

authored_hints.Hint1: Press `Run` then try to click on the bouncing balls!

game_variables_intro_choices_d_independe.bubble_choice_description:
**Difficulty:** 🌶️🌶️🌶️

**Game Mechanic:** Chase

**How to win:** Freeze all 5 opponents. When you tag an opponent they freeze. But they can unfreeze each other!
game_variables_intro_choices_d_independe.long_instructions:
**Play the game!**
 
 - Press `Run` and play the game. 
 
 - What do you notice?
 - Press `Finish` to play another game or move on.

authored_hints.Hint1: It's freeze tag. Use the arrows and chase the wizzards!

panels.games_dev_variables_panel-c730ee2c-1d3b-:
## Use variables in a game!

Variables are like boxes with labels that store information.

The information inside these boxes while the game is being played, helping you keep track of important details throughout the game.
panels.games_dev_variables_panel-f291fa1b-ba53-: # STEP 1️⃣ ## Show the variable Put the variable name and current value onto the play space. Use the location pin to choose where it goes.
panels.games_dev_variables_panel-a8841bd0-4808-: # STEP 2️⃣ ## Set variable Set the value of the variable with this block. What should the starting score be?
panels.games_dev_variables_panel-68fde408-b74c-: # STEP 3️⃣ ## Change variable This block changes the value of the variable. For example, you could add points to the score variable or decrease the value of a health variable.

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Set the score to 0 and show the score variable in the playspace.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**Scoreboard Setup**

1.  Add `show variable score` block to the `when run` program

2.  Then connect a `set score to 0` block 

3.  Press `Run` 

Next, we'll learn how to change the score!

authored_hints.Hint1:
This block will show your variable on the playspace:
[blockly block][0]
authored_hints.Hint2:
The starting value of your variable is set using this block: 
[blockly block][0]

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Change the score by 1 and check that the scoreboard updated.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**Click the sprite to change the score.**

1.  Add a `change score by 1` under the event `when sprite clicked`  

2.  Click the coin after pressing `Run`.  Does the scoreboard change?

authored_hints.Hint1:
The score will change by adding this block **under** the event:

[blockly block][0]

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Set the `health` variable to change by -1 every time the bomb sprite is clicked.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**Health decreases when the sprite is clicked.**

1.  Add a `change health by -1` under the event `when sprite clicked` 

2.  Press `Run` and watch the variable change when you click on the sprite

authored_hints.Hint1:
This time the variable should decrease when the player sprite touches the enemy sprite. To decrease the value, you will use a negative number.
[blockly block][0]

dsls.markdown: Look at these coding blocks using variables. Match the correct code to the animation.
dsls.title: Match the code to the animation

panels.games_dev_variables_panel-5fea9700-21a2-: # STEP 4️⃣ ## Check the value This block checks to see if the condition is true. If it is true it will run the code inside.

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Make an effect happen when the player scores 5 points.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**How do you win?** Check if the player has a winning score.

1.  Connect the `if score = 3` block at the end of the `when sprite clicked` program

2.  Add a `do starburst effect block` _inside_ the new block

3.  Press `Run` then click on the coin. Can you win?

authored_hints.Hint1:
This conditional block will check to see if the value of the variable equals a certain value.
[blockly block][0]

dsls.markdown: Look at these coding blocks using conditionals. Match the correct code to the picture.
dsls.title: Match the code to the animation

game_variables_fix_choices_d_independent.bubble_choice_description:
**Difficulty:** 🌶️

**Game Mechanics:** Collect & Avoid

**Problem:** We don't know what the score is!
game_variables_fix_choices_d_independent.short_instructions: Update the scoreboard to show the number of coins you collected.
game_variables_fix_choices_d_independent.long_instructions:
##Game Mechanic: COLLECT
**Problem:** How many coins do I have?

**Fix:** Add the scoreboard with the block `show coins`

authored_hints.Hint1: We need to `show variable` in order for the user to know how many coins they have collected!

game_variables_fix_choices_e_independent.bubble_choice_description:
**Difficulty:** 🌶️

**Game Mechanics:** Chasing

**Problem:** The bunny moves too slow to catch the carrot!
game_variables_fix_choices_e_independent.short_instructions: Increase the rabbit's speed to 3 or more so it can catch the carrot!
game_variables_fix_choices_e_independent.long_instructions:
##Game Mechanic: CHASING
**Problem:** The rabbit is too slow to get the carrot!

**Fix:** Change the rabbit's speed by updating the `speed` variable to 3 or faster.

authored_hints.Hint1:
Look for the speed variable at the top of the program. Try changing the value.
<xml>
<block xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml" type="variables_set" id="J?iD?dAxk:5a|{3*vPkd" deletable="false">
  <field name="VAR" id="r[GkJc@uY0o`[{8pH_Om">speed</field>
  <value name="VALUE">
    <block type="math_number" id="oeoAo)P9r?JyFZaYez^P" deletable="false">
      <field name="NUM">1</field>
    </block>
  </value>
</block>
</xml>

game_variables_fix_choices_f_independent.bubble_choice_description:
**Difficulty:** 🌶️

**Game Mechanics:** Avoiding

**Problem:** The crab is too hard to escape!
game_variables_fix_choices_f_independent.short_instructions: Adjust the crab's speed by setting the difficultyLevel to 3 or 4.
game_variables_fix_choices_f_independent.long_instructions:
##Game Mechanic: AVOIDING
**Problem:** The crab moves too fast.

**Fix:** Adjust the crab's speed by setting the `difficultyLevel` to 3 or 4.

game_variables_fix_choices_g_independent.bubble_choice_description:
**Difficulty:** 🌶️

**Game Mechanics:** Collect

**Problem:** The hay bundle is too hard to catch!
game_variables_fix_choices_g_independent.short_instructions: Adjust the hay bundle's speed by setting the `difficultyLevel` to 3 or 4.
game_variables_fix_choices_g_independent.long_instructions:
##Game Mechanic: COLLECT
**Problem:** The bundle moves too fast.

**Fix:** Adjust the hay bundle's speed by setting the `difficultyLevel` to 3 or 4.

authored_hints.Hint1:
To change the speed of the hay bundle, look for the variable named `difficultyLevel` near the top of the program and try changing the value. 
<xml>
<block xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml" type="variables_set" id="0po+N0fQV1d@mGOMvfn2" deletable="false">
  <field name="VAR" id="GCYV(/}|5rKLotga]K0U">difficultyLevel</field>
  <value name="VALUE">
    <block type="math_number" id=":fx{-4c^^pOg:L}Ynr%G" deletable="false">
      <field name="NUM">15</field>
    </block>
  </value>
</block>
</xml>

game_variables_fix_choices_c_independent.bubble_choice_description:
**Difficulty:** 🌶️🌶️

**Game Mechanics:** Click & Collect 

**Problem:** Nothing happens if the player wins
game_variables_fix_choices_c_independent.short_instructions: Add to your program so something happens if a player wins the game!
game_variables_fix_choices_c_independent.long_instructions:
**Fix the game!**
 
1. Press `Run` and try out the game

- Program something to happen if a player wins the game
- Click `Finish` when you are finished

***

***Hint:*** *In level 6 we learned how to check if the variable equals winning condition.*

authored_hints.Hint1:
Did you add a conditional to one of your events?
<xml>
<block xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml" type="gamelab_ifVarEquals" id="M/gCD$xcQAi@;(xYGB%;">
  <field name="NUM" id="TPks;#qQ]AsS/}K=XrX$">ballCount</field>
  <value name="VAL">
    <block type="math_number" id="4k.$ts7E5)WO(|_4LA29">
      <field name="NUM">8</field>
    </block>
  </value>
</block>
</xml>
authored_hints.Hint2: You can code anything to happen when the ballCount reaches 8! Just nest the block you want to happen **within** the `if ballCount = 8` block.

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: It's time to create whatever you want!
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**Practice designing and developing your own game!** Now you can start creating whatever you want.

1.  Setup your game with player sprites and a background
2.  Program the sprites to move with behaviors
3.  Design or upload your own characters by clicking the _costumes_ tab
4.  Make the variable change using events
5.  Check if the player has a winning score, then code a celebration

authored_hints.hint1: **It's time to get creative.** Use the checklist in the instructions to get started.

callouts.costumes_tab: Click here to add costumes to your project.

dsls.description: Choose a game to check out how environment type sprites block all of the others!

game_mechanics_type_choice1_independent.short_instructions: Try out this game! Use the arrows to move the astronaut to find their friend.
game_mechanics_type_choice1_independent.long_instructions:
**Try it out!**
- Move the astronaut to find their friend
- The moon sprites block the player so you'll have to go around them

- Press `Finish` when you are ready to move on

game_new3_environment_choice1_independen.short_instructions: Try out this game! Use the arrows to move the dog to collect the bones.
game_new3_environment_choice1_independen.long_instructions:
**Try it out!**

- Move the dog to collect the bones 

- Watch out for the cactus!
- Press `Finish` when you're ready to move on

game_new3_environment_choice2_independen.short_instructions: Try out this game! Use the arrows to pig to get the corn.
game_new3_environment_choice2_independen.long_instructions:
**Try it out!**

- Move the pig to get the corn

- The grass sprites block the pig so you will have to stay on the dirt path

- Press `Finish` when you are ready to move on

panels.game_new_panel-dcd59a3f-edf7-45b3-af02-d: #### Sprite types Setting a sprite’s type determines how it interacts with other sprites. Watch this game in action. **What do you notice about the three sprites?**
panels.game_new_panel-e5efed81-986d-4e21-8002-f: #### Environment sprites When sprites are set to environment, they stop all other sprites from moving.

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Use the new `set sprite type to environment` block so the wall sprites block the player.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**Set the wall sprite type to environment to make this game more challenging.**

1.  Add the `set sprite type to environment` block to `when run`. 

2.  Press `Run` and use the arrow keys to move the player. 
    -   Are the wall sprites blocking the player?

authored_hints.Hint1:
With this new block you can set a sprite's type. Environment sprites won't allow other sprites to pass through them.
[blockly block][0]

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Use the new `set sprite type to environment` block so the wall sprites block the enemies!
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**Set the walls sprite type to environment to block the enemies.**

1.  Use the `set sprite type to environment` under `when run`.

2.  Press `Run` and use the arrow keys to move the player. 
    -   Are the wall sprites blocking the enemies?

authored_hints.Hint1: [blockly block][0]

panels.game_design_spr_types-6451ed33-47e5-458c: ### **Player sprites** Player sprites gradually fall downwards (as if there was gravity!)
panels.game_design_spr_types-53c92e2f-0462-48a7: ### **Player sprites** When sprites are set to player, they cannot move past environment sprites (but can stand on them!)
panels.game_design_spr_types-4936be88-574e-4dd8: ### **Player sprites** Player sprites can be programmed to jump (but cannot automatically do it).

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Explore what happens when we apply gravity! Click `Finish` when you're ready to continue on.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**Set the sprite's type to player.**

1.  Add the `set sprite type to player`.

2.  The player sprite follows the rules of gravity, and it can't move past `environment` type sprites.

3.  Click `Run` and watch the player drop and land on the ground

authored_hints.Hint1:
Practice setting the sprite's type to **player.**
[blockly block][0]

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Program the player to `begin moving with arrow keys` and `jump`. Choose the jump size.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**Make your player jump!**

1.  Add a `make player do jump` block to the `when up pressed` event and choose the jump size

2.  Press `Run` then tap the arrows to move and jump!

authored_hints.Hint2: There is a new block that makes any player type sprites jump! Attach it to the when up pressed event. [blockly block][0]

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: Explore what happens when we apply gravity! Click `Finish` when you're ready to continue on.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**Set the sprite types.**

1.  Add a `set alien sprite type to player` block to the end of program

2.  Add a `set stone sprite type to environment` to the end of the program

3.  Click `Run` and watch the player drop and land on the ground

authored_hints.Hint1:
Practice setting the sprite's type to **player.**
[blockly block][0]

dsls.markdown: Look at these coding blocks using variables. Match the correct code to the animation.
dsls.title: Match the code to the animation

game_development_try_it_independent.bubble_choice_description:
**Difficulty:** 🌶️

**Problem:** The penguin player can not reach the gems!
game_development_try_it_independent.short_instructions: Try out this game! Press up to jump and use the arrows to move. Collect 5 gems to win.
game_development_try_it_independent.long_instructions:
##Debugging Practice
**Problem:** The player can't jump high enough to get the gems.

**Fix:** Change the size of the player's jump.

game_development_environ_independent.bubble_choice_description:
**Difficulty:** 🌶️🌶️

**Problem:** The maze of clouds keeps falling away!
game_development_environ_independent.short_instructions: Add another `set sprite type to environment` so the monster can land on the green platforms.
game_development_environ_independent.long_instructions:
##Debugging Practice
**Problem:** The maze of clouds falls down!

**Fix:** Make sure the sprites are all set to the correct type.

authored_hints.Hint1:
The clouds are falling like player sprites. Set the clouds type to environment instead. 
<xml>
<block xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml" type="GameDev_setGroup" id="s0~~e0!}K[fm;ShKs2sG">
  <field name="GROUP">"walls"</field>
  <value name="SPRITE">
    <block type="gamelab_allSpritesWithAnimation" id="x,sMj}}CfvGtzA+U2wfm">
      <field name="ANIMATION">"ground_grass"</field>
    </block>
  </value>
</block>
</xml>

game_development_player_independent.short_instructions: Add another `set sprite type to environment` so the monster can land on the green platforms.
game_development_player_independent.long_instructions:
##Debugging Practice
**Problem:** The mermaid isn't jumping to get to her friend. 

**Fix:** Make sure the sprites are all set to the correct type. A sprite must be set to the type player to jump.

authored_hints.Hint1:
The clouds are falling like player sprites. Set the clouds type to environment instead. 
<xml>
<block xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml" type="GameDev_setGroup" id="s0~~e0!}K[fm;ShKs2sG">
  <field name="GROUP">"walls"</field>
  <value name="SPRITE">
    <block type="gamelab_allSpritesWithAnimation" id="x,sMj}}CfvGtzA+U2wfm">
      <field name="ANIMATION">"ground_grass"</field>
    </block>
  </value>
</block>
</xml>

game_development_platform_independent.bubble_choice_description:
**Difficulty:** 🌶️🌶️🌶️

**Problem:** The monster falls through the green platforms!
game_development_platform_independent.short_instructions: Add another `set sprite type to environment` so the monster can land on the green platforms.
game_development_platform_independent.long_instructions:
##Debugging Practice
**Problem:** The monster falls through the green platforms.

**Fix:** Add another `set sprite type to environment` for the green platforms.

authored_hints.Hint1:
The gray platforms are set to the correct type, but you need to add this code so the green platforms are also environment types. 
<xml>
<block xmlns="https://developers.google.com/blockly/xml" type="GameDev_setGroup" id="s0~~e0!}K[fm;ShKs2sG">
  <field name="GROUP">"walls"</field>
  <value name="SPRITE">
    <block type="gamelab_allSpritesWithAnimation" id="x,sMj}}CfvGtzA+U2wfm">
      <field name="ANIMATION">"ground_grass"</field>
    </block>
  </value>
</block>
</xml>

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
## Game Jam Theme Generator

### Click on each character to roll the dice that selects your theme!

dsls.markdown:
##Make a quick plan
Answer the following questions in your activity guide:
1. **What is the theme of your game?** - Adjective: ________ - Noun: ________ - Game Mechanic: ________
2. **Decide on sprites.** - Main Character costume: ________ - Collectible or Enemy costume: ________ - (Optional) Other: ________
3. **What is the objective of the game?** ________ 4. **Winning Condition** - How does the player win the game? ________ - What will you need to keep track of? ________
5. **[Optional] Losing Condition** - How does the player lose the game? ________ - What will you need to keep track of? ________

https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.short_instructions: You have reached the end of the module! Create your own GameDev project.
https://studio.code.org/s/elem-game-desi.long_instructions:
**Develop your game!**

1.  Set up the environment

2.  Add the player sprite & code their movement

3.  Add the collectible or enemy sprite(s) & code their movement

4.  Add your variable & code it to change with an event

5.  Add a winning and/or losing condition

authored_hints.Hint1: You will find all of the blocks organized into categories in the toolbox.

dsls.markdown:
##Time to play some games!
After you try out each game, answer the following questions in your activity guide:
1. **How fun was the game?** 2. **How challenging was the game?** 3. **Was it easy to figure out how to play?** 4. **Did the scoreboard work?** 5. **What was the label of the variable(s)?** 6. **Glows - What is great about this game?** 7. **Grows - What should the developers improve?**
